TextENGNew: After walking for a while down the single road cutting through the forest, Miyuki-chan suddenly stopped.
"...What's wrong?"
In response to my question, she silently pointed ahead.
A single car was parked at the base of what appeared to be the long stone stairway leading up to the shrine.
changeReason: New adds 'what appeared to be' and 'long,' more faithfully rendering Japanese であろう (presumably) and 長い omitted in old.

TextENGNew: This car didn't seem to be from the area either. The license plate read 'Nerima' — most likely someone who'd driven here from Tokyo.
"What the...?@k@vS10/62/410100291. Don't tell me... again?"
changeReason: Old restructures Japanese まさか…また？ (Don't tell me... again?) into declarative 'Again...? That's impossible'; new preserves the questioning tone.

TextENGNew: Could someone be dead inside the car — I swallowed those words before they could leave my mouth. ...I was afraid that speaking them aloud might make them real.
"I'll go take a look."
"Ah, I'll come too."
For a split second, the faces of 'those people' we'd seen up close just moments ago flashed through my mind.
Three men and women, dead as if in peaceful sleep, with serene expressions on their faces.
(I never want to see something like that again...)
With something close to prayer in my heart, I cautiously peered into the car.
Please, let there be no dead bodies inside...!
I shone my flashlight inside. ...No one was there.
"...Thank god. Nobody's inside."
"......@k@vS10/40/410300269. That's strange, though — no one being inside."
I'd been about to smile with relief, but Miyuki-chan turned a stern expression my way. She then swept her light under and around the car, as if confirming something.
changeReason: Old incorrectly attributes shining the light to narrator; Japanese subject is Miyuki-chan.

TextENGNew: ...There was no sign of anyone at all.
"Hey, Miyuki-chan.@k@vS10/62/410100297. Why is it strange that nobody's inside?"
"...This car feels like it only just arrived today.@k@vS10/40/410300271. If that's the case... where did the person driving it go?"
"Look at the tire tracks, see?"
At Miyuki-chan's prompting, I aimed my flashlight downward.
Sure enough, behind the tires were fresh ruts carved sharply into the muddy ground.
The rain had started falling in the evening, so these tracks could only have been made after that.
"The car has ordinary plates.@k@vS10/40/410300276. ...If it didn't, that would settle it."
changeReason: Old misreads as uncertain question; new correctly renders as statement with conditional.

TextENGNew: "Huh?@k@vS10/62/410100300. Miyuki-chan, what did you just say?"
"...It's nothing, never mind."
Miyuki-chan swallowed the rest of her words and turned her light back toward the car's interior.
She meticulously swept her flashlight over every corner of the driver's seat and passenger seat. Once she finished, she moved on to illuminating the back seat.
The back seat was littered with magazines and CD cases, but nothing seemed particularly unusual.
"Hey, Miyuki-chan.@k@vS10/62/410100302. What were you about to say earlier?"
"It's nothing important.@k@vS10/40/410300279. ...Please don't worry about it."
Even as she said that, Miyuki-chan's eyes continued darting around the car's interior.
Despite claiming it was 'nothing important,' she was examining everything with painstaking thoroughness.
We'd already confirmed no one was inside — so what was she looking for now...?
Feeling awkward just standing there, I started checking the car myself. But I couldn't find anything out of the ordinary.
A keychain with a cute little mascot character dangled from the ignition in the driver's seat.
(Is the driver a woman...?)
Then again, plenty of men carry cute accessories like that these days. No point jumping to conclusions.
"Nothing seems out of the ordinary.@k@vS10/40/410300281. ...Shall we move on?"
"Ah, yeah..."
At Miyuki-chan's urging, I stepped away from the car.
"Anyway, where exactly are we right now?"
"We're just in front of Furude Shrine right now.@k@vS10/40/410300283. ...Look, right here."
When had she pulled that out? Miyuki-chan unfolded a map, illuminated it with her flashlight, and slid her finger along it before stopping at a single point.
"You can see the stone steps just ahead, right?@k@vS10/40/410300285. The shrine is at the top of those stairs."
"Stairs..."
When she said that, I looked up, and sure enough — even in the darkness, I could faintly make out a steep staircase stretching straight upward.
But the torii gate that should have been at the top was invisible from here.
changeReason: Old translates 鳥居 as generic 'shrine gate'; new uses the standard English term 'torii gate.'

TextENGNew: "We have to climb that thing?@k@vS10/62/410100309. How many steps are there...?"
"Regardless, climbing is our only option.@k@vS10/40/410300287. Let's go, Ryuunosuke-san."
"Oh, right..."
Miyuki-chan walked forward with confident steps and began ascending the stone stairs without hesitation. I hurried after her, taking them two at a time.
"--Whoa!"
But the poor visibility and uneven footing tripped me up, and I ended up catching myself on the steps with both hands.
"Are you all right?@k@vS10/40/410300289. It's slippery, so please be careful."
"Yeah, sorry..."
From a few steps above, Miyuki-chan extended her umbrella over me where I'd stumbled.
changeReason: Old says Miyuki 'came down' but Japanese indicates she stayed above.

TextENGNew: I could barely see my footing, the rain made everything slick, and some of the stone steps were coated in a treacherous layer of moss.
How does Miyuki-chan climb these things so easily...?
The light rain continued to fall, leaving us both thoroughly soaked.
"Achoo!"
"Are you okay?@k@vS10/40/410300291. It's gotten quite cold. Let's hurry."
How pathetic, to be the one getting worried about. ...Which one of us was supposed to be leading, again?
"Are you holding up all right, Miyuki-chan?@k@vS10/62/410100315. Aren't you cold?"
"I'm perfectly fine.@k@vS10/40/410300293. Ryuunosuke-san, there's a lot of moss right here.@k@vS10/40/410300294. Watch your step so you don't slip."
"R-roger that... whoa."
Even as I answered, the earlier slip had left me hunched over, walking with an embarrassingly cautious gait.
changeReason: Old misreads as another slip; Japanese says the earlier slip made him walk cautiously.

TextENGNew: In stark contrast, Miyuki-chan moved with steady, assured steps, as if she were walking a familiar path.
I squinted at her feet and noticed she was wearing trekking boots that laced up to the ankle.
She had clearly dressed with rough terrain in mind. ...All that Girl Scout experience wasn't just for show.
After climbing dozens upon dozens of steps, the shrine's torii gate finally came into view.
It might once have been a dignified structure, but years of exposure to the elements without any upkeep had left it darkened and pitifully decrepit.
"It's not going to collapse on us or anything, is it...?"
I looked up nervously at the torii gate as I continued walking.
changeReason: Old translates 鳥居 as generic 'shrine gate'; new uses the more precise 'torii gate.'

TextENGNew: Just then--.
*splash*...
I heard a single footstep from somewhere behind me.
I spun around. ...No one was there.
"What's wrong?"
Perhaps sensing something odd about my stopping, Miyuki-chan — who had already passed through the torii gate ahead of me — turned back and called out. ...But I gave only a vague reply.
...Miyuki-chan was walking ahead of me. So those footsteps couldn't have been hers.
(...Just my imagination.)
Telling myself that, I passed through the torii gate and began walking across the shrine grounds.
*splash*,@k@|@y *splash*,@k@|@y *splash*...
There it was again.
Miyuki-chan and I should be the only ones walking through these grounds...
Then what on earth are these footsteps?!
"That building up ahead — that's the main hall."
"...Ah, right.@k@vS10/62/410100326. There shouldn't be anyone there... right?"
"...Let's check, just to be safe."
With that, Miyuki-chan headed toward the offertory box. I followed close behind.
*splash*,@k@|@y *splash*,@k@|@y *splash*...
As if to mock my growing unease, the footsteps continued to echo.
...Maybe it was just our own footsteps reverberating.
Even so, I kept glancing behind me as I walked. There was definitely some kind of presence — I could feel it.
*splash*,@k@|@y *splash*,@k@|@y *splash*,@k@|@y *sploosh*.
"Whoa--"
I stepped right into a puddle. I'd been so focused on what was behind me that I'd neglected to watch where I was going...

changeReason: Old 'Oh ho ho, how stupid of me' mismatches Japanese とほほ散々だよ (frustration); new 'Ah, damn it all...' better captures the exasperated tone.

TextENGNew: Letting out a miserable sigh, I dropped my gaze to the puddle at my feet... and froze.
My feet were sunk in a pool of bright red blood...!
And at my feet — was that a person lying there...?!
It was a girl.
The corpse of a young girl — her abdomen cruelly torn open, lying there with glassy eyes staring vacantly into the void.
A swarm of black shadows — the silhouettes of crow feathers — crowded thickly around her.
changeReason: Old adds 'partially obscured' not in Japanese; new 'crowded thickly around her' more faithfully renders おびたたしく群がる.

TextENGNew: And reverberating all around, the droning voices of old men chanting sutras.
changeReason: Old translates 念仏 as generic 'prayer'; new 'sutras' is more accurate for Buddhist chanting (念仏).

TextENGNew: It was as if... as if...!
"Ryuunosuke-san...?"
Miyuki-chan noticed something was wrong and turned back toward me.
"What happened?"
"Miyuki-chan... this... is..."
"This...?"
Looking puzzled, Miyuki-chan aimed her flashlight at my feet.
But there was nothing there — only a puddle.
The girl who had been lying there just moments ago was gone.
I stared hard at the puddle again, then checked the cuffs of my pants.
They were completely soaked — but what drenched them wasn't blood.
"Ryuunosuke-san, are you all right?"
"...Y-yeah."
The rain intensified, countless droplets hammering the ground with force.
Thick clouds blanketed the sky, and the entire area was sealed in darkness... I couldn't see a thing.
Having regained a sliver of composure, I carefully swept my flashlight around my surroundings... but nothing seemed out of place.
"What's the matter, Ryuunosuke-san...?@k@vS10/40/410300304. Your face is white as a sheet."
"Oh, uh. No, it's nothing... Haha.@k@vS10/62/410100337. Think I just saw things!"
"Saw things...?"
Miyuki-chan's expression turned grave, and she began scanning our surroundings intently.
The pounding rain seemed to erase every trace, every presence.
"What kind of hallucination?@k@vS10/40/410300307. A person? Or--"
"Ah, no... sorry.@k@vS10/62/410100339. It was probably... just my imagination."
Maybe a faint glow filtering through the clouds had reflected off the torii gate or something, and that reflection in the puddle had tricked my eyes.
But... even so, what about that red blood? And what was that girl's corpse lying on the ground...?
Was it truly a hallucination...?
"Ryuunosuke-san.@k@vS10/40/410300309. Do you hallucinate often?"
"...Of course not!@k@vS10/62/410100341_1. I don't live the kind of life that would make me see things."
"I... see.@k@vS10/40/410300311. That is rather unsettling, though."
The words caught in my throat, and I couldn't say anything.
Maybe the 'vision' I'd seen earlier was still lingering in my mind...
"...No sign of them, I suppose."
"No... it's nothing."
Miyuki-chan said that, and then, for just an instant--
"--Did they beat us here... hehe..."
A cruel smile played across her lips... and her eyes flashed with a sharp, cold glint--.
That laugh just now... what was that...?
I couldn't understand what Miyuki-chan meant at all. And what did she mean by 'them'...?
I looked down at the puddle again.
No matter how many times I shone my flashlight on it, nothing was reflected on the water's surface anymore.
"Let's go, Ryuunosuke-san."
"Oh, right..."
That laugh earlier... what was that about?
Ever since we arrived in Hinamizawa, strange things had started happening.
And being steeped in this malevolent atmosphere had... changed her...?
changeReason: Old translates 凶気にまみれた as 'unfortunate events'; new 'steeped in this malevolent atmosphere' correctly captures the supernatural 凶気.

TextENGNew: If that was the case... would I be next?
(No way... that can't be...)
I started walking forward, as if to shake off the ridiculous thoughts running through my head.
Just then, I noticed a large, half-torn paper lantern lying on the ground with characters written on it.
"Wata...nagashi?"
"That's the village festival of Hinamizawa.@k@vS10/40/410300318. It seems the festival was held right here."
"On the shrine grounds, huh.@k@vS10/62/410100350. Must have been pretty lively back in the day."
"This place seems to have served as a kind of community center.@k@vS10/40/410300320. I'm sure this assembly hall was used for village events, lessons, that sort of thing."
"Huh~. I see..."
I said that, but it wasn't that I was impressed by Miyuki-chan's explanation. ...It was that I hadn't known any of this before.
changeReason: Old completely reverses meaning: says 'I knew all that' when Japanese says 'I hadn't known any of this.'

TextENGNew: Of course, it wasn't necessarily strange that she might know things I didn't.
...It might sound arrogant, but I prided myself on having researched Hinamizawa more thoroughly than most reporters.
So where had this woman, Sorimachi Miyuki, obtained such a wealth of information that far surpassed my own...?
changeReason: Old omits comparison to other reporters in Japanese そこかしこの記者よりも; new correctly includes 'more thoroughly than most reporters.'

TextENGNew: Moreover, Miyuki-chan described it all as casually as a local recounting yesterday's events.
As if a resident of this village were giving a guided tour to an outsider...
I felt guilty for thinking it, but... a growing suspicion was taking root inside me.
While I stood there lost in thought, Miyuki-chan finished offering her prayer and circled through the shrine grounds toward the assembly hall in the back.
changeReason: New captures Japanese そんな俺を差し置いて (leaving distracted narrator aside) and ぐるりと回る (circled through), both omitted in old.

TextENGNew: "The assembly hall should be this way."
"? ...What's wrong, Ryuunosuke-san?"
"Miyuki-chan... you seem to know an awful lot about this village."
"Well, yes...@k@vS10/40/410300324. But it's all secondhand, so I don't really know that much."
How much of that was true? ...The swelling doubt felt like it was going to crush my chest.
As we rounded the corner, Miyuki-chan and I both stopped in our tracks.
...Light was leaking from the doorway of the assembly hall — a place where no one should be.
"Someone's in there..."
"...Looks like it.@k@vS10/62/410100356. Could it be... the owner of that car from earlier...?"
"Well... who knows..."
Miyuki-chan and I tried to get a read on what was inside, but we couldn't make out much from here.
"That light... could it be the one I saw from the hilltop earlier...?"
"...Probably."
"...Shouldn't we observe for a while first?"
"Observe...? You mean you're saying we shouldn't go in?"
"Right. That would be--"
*CLATTER*--!
Something crashed inside the assembly hall.
Then came a woman's voice.
I couldn't make out what she was saying.
"...Let's go take a look."
"Go take a look...?"
"Inside the assembly hall.@k@vS10/40/410300331. It can't hurt to check, right?"
With that, Miyuki-chan placed her hand on the assembly hall door.
I hurriedly grabbed her hand to stop her.
"W-wait a second!"
"...What if it's a ghost or something?!"
"...Excuse me?"
Miyuki-chan let out a small sigh and fixed me with a steady look. ...Her expression was one of mild exasperation, clearly saying 'What are you even talking about?'
"Oh, I mean...@k@vS10/62/410100365. What I'm saying is..."
"...Well, I suppose that is a possibility.@k@vS10/40/410300335. But we won't know whether it's a ghost or not unless we check, will we?"
"Well, true, but..."
...It's not that I was scared, mind you.
I just thought that being cautious was the wisest course of action in a situation like this.
...So I'm not scared! Absolutely, definitely not scared!!
"If it's a ghost, I'll punch it for you.@k@vS10/40/410300337. Here goes... I'm opening the door!"
"Ah, hey--"
But my protests were completely in vain.
With impressive boldness, Miyuki-chan threw open the assembly hall door in one swift motion.
*SLIDE*--.
In that same instant, something was pressed against my Adam's apple.
changeReason: Old translates 喉仏 as generic 'throat'; new correctly specifies 'Adam's apple.'

TextENGNew: The next moment, I was shoved from the side. ...It was Miyuki-chan.
As I staggered, the sleeve of a kimono flashed across my field of vision.
Rubbing the spot on my hip where I'd hit the ground, I suddenly remembered the situation and looked up with dread.
In the shadow of the doorway — a blind spot from the entrance — a woman in a kimono was standing there.
Something about her figure exuded an eerie, otherworldly aura.
changeReason: Old uses 'magical atmosphere' for 妖しい雰囲気; new 'eerie, otherworldly aura' more accurately captures the unsettling supernatural connotation.

TextENGNew: A ghost--.
The word nearly left my lips, but I couldn't bring myself to say it.
She was pointing the tip of a long, slender cylindrical object straight at Miyuki-chan.
Was this what had been pressed against my throat a moment ago...?!
"M-Miyuki-chan...?"
Miyuki-chan stood directly in front of the woman, staring her down without flinching.
The woman and Miyuki-chan faced each other for some time without moving a muscle... and it was the woman who spoke first.
"--Who are you people?"
"O-oh, um..."
Her low, suppressed voice carried an unmistakable intensity.
changeReason: Old 'deep, powerful voice left me awestricken' misses 押し殺した (suppressed); new correctly renders the restrained menace.

TextENGNew: I shrank back. But Miyuki-chan didn't waver for an instant.
"...In situations like this, isn't it proper manners to introduce yourself first?"
"We're not anyone suspicious.@k@vS10/40/410300343. Could you... lower that, please?"
changeReason: Old 'nothing to be afraid of' misinterprets 怪しいものじゃない (not suspicious); new correctly renders 'not anyone suspicious.'

TextENGNew: "...Hmph."
The woman let out a breath, then withdrew the slender object and tucked it under her arm.
She glanced back and forth between Miyuki-chan and me with sharp eyes, then her expression softened slightly.
"What's a guy like you doing here with such a fine young lady, I wonder?"
"Er... what is that supposed to mean?"
The kimono-clad woman didn't answer, merely letting out a suppressed chuckle.
And Miyuki-chan, for her part, seemed completely unfazed by the woman's cryptic reaction.
"Sorry for barging in so late, but we need to make a phone call.@k@vS10/40/410300345. Would that be all right?"
"Phone call...?"

"Ahahahaha, what a foolish thing to say.@k@vS10/43/410400009. Did you really think there were working phones in this village?@k@vS10/43/410400010. Where do you think you are?@k@vS10/43/410400011. You must know, Hinamizawa is a ghost town."
"...That's true.@k@vS10/40/410300347. But we thought it was worth a try."
"Sorry to disappoint.@k@vS10/43/410400013. Well, I'm not actually sorry."
The tension drained from the kimono-clad woman, and she spoke in a relaxed, teasing tone.
She looked to be close to 40. An inscrutable aura emanated from her entire being, lending her a weighty, commanding presence.
Her complexion was smooth and healthy, but her deeply chiseled features suggested a life that hadn't followed any simple path.
"Anyway, come on in.@k@vS10/43/410400015. Standing around dripping wet like that — you'll catch cold, you know?"
"Miyuki-chan...?"
"...Well then, we'll take you up on that."
At the woman's urging, Miyuki-chan and I stepped inside the assembly hall.
The crumbling wooden assembly hall reeked faintly of mold. Rain and wind gusted in through a broken window.
Papers and notices were stuck to various walls and pillars. Though faded, I could make out phrases like "Onigafuchi Defense Alliance" and "Oppose Dam Construction."
changeReason: Old translates 鬼ヶ淵死守同盟 as 'Onigafuchi Guardians'; new 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' is more faithful to 死守同盟. Also ダム建設反対 rendered as proper slogan in new.

TextENGNew: I'd read about the dam construction plans for this village in old newspapers and reference materials.

The plan would have submerged Hinamizawa beneath a reservoir, but opposition escalated into murder, and the project was abandoned. Then the Great Hinamizawa Disaster struck, and the whole matter simply vanished.
changeReason: Old says plan was 'suspended' but Japanese 頓挫 means abandoned/failed.

TextENGNew: I tried to imagine the villagers gathered here, plastering these posters on the walls, debating passionately against the dam — but the image didn't quite come together.
Strikes, labor movements, the struggles of the Showa era — they felt as distant to me as the Industrial Revolution or the dawn of civilization.
Stepping into a space steeped in relics of a bygone era filled me with a strange feeling.
It was as though I'd wandered into another world entirely.
"This way."
At her prompting, the kimono-clad woman flung open a sliding door at the end of the hallway.
A meeting room lined with tattered tatami mats appeared before us — and I let out a small gasp.
The people inside the room looked up at us with hollow, listless eyes.
changeReason: Old says 'a man' (singular) but Japanese refers to multiple people.

TextENGNew: So the woman in the kimono wasn't the only one here after all.
...Standing around like this wasn't going to accomplish anything. I took a step into the room, nudging Miyuki-chan to follow.
"Good evening.@k@vS10/40/410300351. Sorry for the disturbance~."
...But Miyuki-chan proved far bolder than me — she breezed past me into the room and greeted everyone in a lively, cheerful voice that felt completely out of place.
Left with no choice, I timidly followed after her.
Inside the room were a young man who looked about twenty, and a young woman who appeared the same age or perhaps slightly older.
They were staring at us with wary, suspicious eyes.
Looking more carefully, there was one more person — a man sleeping under a futon in the corner of the room.
Including the kimono-clad woman, that made four people here in total...
None of them stood up or returned our greeting. They just stared blankly at us.
Ghostlike... lifeless eyes.
The eeriness made me instinctively turn my face away... only to be startled again.
Behind her bright greeting, Miyuki-chan had been observing the people in the room with sharp, almost predatory eyes.
"M-Miyuki-cha..."
Perhaps noticing that I was watching her, Miyuki-chan instantly switched back to a bright smile.
What on earth... was that face just now...?
"Everyone, sorry to bother you while you're resting.@k@vS10/40/410300354. We came hoping to borrow a phone.@k@vS10/40/410300355. If you don't mind?"
changeReason: Old adds 'important phone call' and 'had to barge in' not in Japanese; new 'came hoping to borrow a phone' is more faithful.

TextENGNew: The assembly hall was in shambles. The tatami mats were falling apart and cobwebs clung to every piece of furniture.
Miyuki-chan scanned the room, then walked over to a toppled public phone in the corner of the assembly hall and let out a disappointed sigh.
"Looks like this is the only phone."
"...Guess so."
A pink public phone lay on the floor covered in a thick layer of dust, its cord severed.
There was no need to pick up the receiver. The chance of that phone still working was zero.
"Maybe there's another one?"
"Unlikely..."
"Just who were you trying to call?"
"Well... the police, actually."
The instant Miyuki-chan said that, the two listless-looking people snapped their heads up in unison.
"Th-the police...? Why?"
"People are dead... we found them on the way here.@k@vS10/62/410100380. They were all sitting in their seats, inside a car..."
"It can't be...!"
Everyone in the room gasped in unison.
...Or rather, not quite everyone. The woman in traditional garb merely flicked her sharp gaze our way for an instant before returning it to the fraying tatami.
changeReason: Old fabricates 'rose to her feet'; Japanese says she merely glanced briefly then returned eyes to tatami.

TextENGNew: "D... did you see it...?"
"See what?"
"Th... the corpses.@k@vS10/41/410200009. Are they all really dead...?"
"Unfortunately, yes."
I mean, of course I could talk about it — I'd seen it with my own eyes.
changeReason: Old reverses meaning: 'I hesitated to talk' but Japanese means 'I can talk about it because I saw it.'

TextENGNew: "They were all dead with peaceful expressions on their faces.@k@vS10/62/410100384. ...What about it?"
changeReason: Old literally translates きれーな顔 as 'beautiful faces,' misleading in death context; new 'peaceful expressions' correctly interprets the phrase.

TextENGNew: "Oh, no...@k@vS10/41/410200012. I'm just not good with dead bodies, myself..."
"I can't imagine anyone is."
"Ah, ahaha...@k@vS10/41/410200014. Th-that's true..."
The young man let out a forced laugh, then looked down. Maybe it was just poor lighting, but his face looked pale.
"Where did you find it...?@k@vS10/42/410600007. The... s... suicide car."
"...It wasn't far from here.@k@vS10/62/410100387. Right, Miyuki-chan?"
"Eh? Right..."
"Miyuki-chan...?"
"Huh?@k@vS10/40/410300361. Oh... We need to contact the police, right away!"
Miyuki-chan's voice was pitched unnaturally high.
changeReason: Old says 'unnaturally loud' but 高い here means 'high-pitched.'

TextENGNew: Had Miyuki-chan been thinking about something completely different this whole time...?
"Have you all... tried your cell phones?"
"We're out of range.@k@vS10/40/410300363. Right, Ryuunosuke-san?"
"R-right..."
"I thought there might be a working phone somewhere in the village, so we came here looking for one..."

When Miyuki-chan said that, the young woman pulled out her cell phone and flipped it open.@k Since it was a different carrier from ours, I felt a spark of hope... but no luck — hers was out of range too.
The kimono-clad woman also fished out a cell phone, held it to her ear to check.@k ...But after a moment, she shrugged and ended the call.
"...Not surprising.@k@vS10/43/410400020. After all, what would the phone connect to in a place like this?"
The young woman tried holding her phone at different angles to catch a signal, then shook her head in resignation.
"Mine too. It's no good.@k@vS10/42/410600010. We're out of range."
Looking more closely at the young woman, she wore a thin-cut top and a fluffy balloon skirt.
changeReason: Old omits the カットソー top and misattributes 'light cotton' to skirt; new correctly lists both garments.

TextENGNew: It was the kind of casual outfit you'd wear on a date around town. She was probably in high heels or sandals to match. Trekking boots were definitely not part of the picture.
There were beer cans and snack bags scattered about in front of her.
Had she been drinking here with the man now passed out in the corner of the room...?
A date... in a place like this? Getting drunk together, no less...?
"Is something wrong?"
"Oh, no. It's nothing."
Perhaps noticing that I'd been staring, the young woman shot me a look tinged with displeasure.
...But she quickly averted her gaze, and with a restless air, reached for a bag of snacks on the floor.
Right beside her, the young man sat hugging his knees, staring fixedly at a single spot on the tatami.
"How about you?"
"Er, huh?"
"Otobe, what happened?@k@vS10/43/410400022. You look frightened..."
The kimono-clad woman idly toyed with her cell phone as she peered at the young man called Otobe. He hunched his shoulders and mumbled something apologetically.
"I-I'm sorry...@k@vS10/41/410200017. I was just doing some thinking..."
"Your phone... Does it work?@k@vS10/40/410300367. Can you connect?"
"R-right... Ok.@k@vS10/41/410200019. Let me turn it on."
"Does it really makes sense to have a cell phone if the power is off...?"
"That's... umm... You're right.@k@vS10/41/410200021. Haha...?!"
As he said that, he glanced toward the kimono-clad woman — and for a brief instant, a startled look crossed his face.
What had he just seen...?
I tried to see what Otobe had been looking at... but the kimono-clad woman's face was in shadow, impossible to make out.
"Hey, you..."
"Sorry.@k@vS10/41/410200023. My battery was running out, so I turned the power off."
As if shutting me out, Otobe turned his back to me and cupped his hands around the phone to hide the screen. Then he carefully worked the buttons while staring at the display.
"There's no way your phone will connect this far in the mountains.@k@vS10/43/410400024. Wouldn't it be better to travel to Okinomiya and talk to someone directly?@k@vS10/43/410400025. Or maybe..."
"...Ah!@k@vS10/41/410200025. My phone has one bar of signal...!"
"Seriously?!"
"Yeah. It looks good.@k@vS10/41/410200027. If I move closer to the entrance, maybe I'll get a better signal..."
Otobe continued staring at his cell phone as he got up to leave the room.
And all the while, Otobe avoided making eye contact with any of us.
...Eventually, I could hear his voice faintly echoing down the hallway.
"Hello? Is this the police?@k@vS10/41/410200029. Yes, yes..."
"Aah... Somehow, his phone was able to connect!"
"It certainly seems that way..."
Miyuki-chan was watching Otobe with a sharp, piercing gaze.
For some reason... she didn't look happy about this at all.
Did she have some reason to be troubled by the phone connecting...?
"Miyuki-chan...?"
When I looked, the kimono-clad woman was also watching Otobe's back with a similarly severe expression.
Otobe's voice was the only sound echoing through the assembly hall.
"In Hinamizawa... Yes...@k@vS10/41/410200031. A group suicide... I heard it from someone else, so I don't know all the details...@k@vS10/41/410200032. Yes... yes..."
Perhaps uncomfortable with all of us watching him, Otobe turned completely away, hunched over, and lowered his voice.
"...Anyway, now that he managed to connect, everything is going to be all right."
"......I hope so."
What was that? Miyuki-chan's words seemed to have some hidden subtext...
Was it just my imagination...?
Just then, I heard Otobe let out an "Ah!"
"What's wrong?"
"My phone... The battery just ran out..."
"I'm sorry..."
Otobe walked back toward us with his phone in hand, looking troubled.
Sure enough, the phone's display was completely dark.
"Even so... you were able to tell the police at least part of the situation...@k@vS10/62/410100399. The police will probably be able to piece it together."
"That's right...!"
...Even as he said that, Otobe's eyes had been flickering anxiously the entire time.
Was this guy really that shy around people? Or was it something else...?
"So now, our only means of contacting the outside world has been cut off."
"...Well, we shouldn't move around much in the middle of the night.@k@|@y@vS10/62/410100401. So, let's just relax and wait for the police to arrive!"
"...I suppose so."
Miyuki let out an exaggerated sigh and returned to the room.
When I sat down on the tatami, the young woman in the balloon skirt shifted aside to make room.
Miyuki-chan took a seat next to her.
"Well now — for five living souls to gather in a village that should be deserted, this must surely be some kind of fate."
changeReason: Old omits 生者 (living people, contrasted with the dead) and ご縁 (fate); new captures both concepts.

TextENGNew: "Then let us enjoy each other's company for this single night, here in this village where only the dead reside."
The kimono-clad woman drew her long, slender object — wrapped in chirimen cloth — close and tucked it under her arm.
Then she picked up a cup of sake, popped off the cap, and thrust it forward.
With no other option, I timidly reached out to accept it. But she swiftly pulled her hand away.
changeReason: Old says 'bottle of sake' but コップ酒 means single-serving cup.

TextENGNew: "This one is for the young lady over there."
She looked straight at Miyuki-chan as she spoke.
There was an unmistakable intensity in her eyes.
Miyuki-chan flashed a fearless grin, accepted the sake, and downed it in one gulp.
changeReason: Old says 'took a sip' but 一息に飲み干した means drank it all in one gulp.

TextENGNew: "Miyuki-chan...?!"
"...Haah, thank you for the hospitality."
"You look like you know how to handle your drink, huh...?"
"Yes, thanks.@k@vS10/40/410300377. ...Let me return the favor."
Miyuki-chan poured another glass of sake and held it out to the woman in the kimono.
"Please."
"Hehe...@k@vS10/43/410400031. If you went to all the trouble, I'll have to oblige."
The woman in the kimono graciously accepted the glass offered to her.
"Can you hold your liquor?"
"Not as well as you."
"Well, the night is young. It may seem long... but not that long!"
The woman in the kimono's sharp eyes looked like they could see through anything.
It felt like her eyes were trying to learn something from Miyuki-chan.
But Miyuki-chan wasn't fazed by the woman's gaze... She was confronting her face to face.
What's the deal with this woman...?
Why is she so concerned with Miyuki-chan...?
And Miyuki-chan is acting the same...
It's like the two of them are old acquaintances...?
"Oh, Ryuunosuke-san.@k@vS10/40/410300381. I'll go get our luggage from the car."
"You can stay here, Miyuki-chan.@k@vS10/62/410100405. I'll handle the luggage..."
"That's all right.@k@vS10/40/410300383. There's a lot of luggage, and I'm sure you won't be able to tell which are proper luggage and which are camping equipment."
"So I'll be going then!"
Miyuki-chan gently smiled to the woman in the kimono, then quickly rose to her feet.
And then, she stormed out of the room in a hurry.
Miyuki-chan was keeping secrets from me...
I was fairly convinced of that.
However, I had no idea what they might be.
And I had no idea why the woman in the kimono was so interested in Miyuki-chan, despite allegedly meeting her for the first time today.
We're in an abandoned village, where all the villagers perished in the disaster. The odds of running into someone you know in a place like this are slim.
Moreover, what are the odds that a freelance writer from Tokyo would be acquainted with this creepy woman in a kimono at all...?
I can't say that it's impossible, but the probability seems quite low.
And what's more, even if the two of them are acquainted, they're not speaking to each other as such.
So, what on earth is going on...?
On top of that, I'm beginning to doubt that Miyuki-chan is a freelance writer at all...
"Then for now, why don't you all introduce yourselves?"
The woman in the kimono looked directly at me as she asked that.
The others followed her lead and started looking at me. Like they were all just waiting for me to speak up.
"Who, me...? That was sudden."
"Shouldn't we go in order?@k@vS10/43/410400036. We all arrived here earlier.@k@vS10/43/410400037. But not all of us know everyone's names."
The young woman in the balloon skirt and the young man named Otobe stared vacantly at me.
And after their gazes fixated on me, I finally realized.
That's it. These people... have no light in their eyes. Like ghosts who've lost their souls...
"What's wrong?@k@vS10/43/410400039. Please hurry up."
And so, at the woman in the kimono's urging... I took a deep breath.
Who are these people...?
I grabbed a can of beer off the floor, pulled the tab, and took a big gulp.
The beer was lukewarm, and one of the most disgusting things I've ever tasted.
